task_completion.description: Agent opened and read the thermal zoning brief on the desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or viewer open showing the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/thermal_zoning_brief.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent created the required IfcZone entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM outliner, spatial tree, or grouping interface showing new IfcZone entities named 'Living Zone', 'Sleeping Zone', and 'Unconditioned Zone'.

task_completion.description: Agent assigned IfcSpace entities (rooms) to the newly created IfcZones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlenderBIM interface showing IfcSpace items nested under, grouped by, or linked to the respective IfcZone entities.

task_completion.description: Agent attached the Pset_ZoneCommon property set to at least one zone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties panel in BlenderBIM showing 'Pset_ZoneCommon' assigned to a selected IfcZone.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the modified IFC project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog or file explorer showing the file 'fzk_thermal_zones.ifc' being saved to the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) graphical interface to create zones and assign spaces.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Blender/Bonsai UI rather than terminal scripts or text editors modifying the IFC file directly.

integrity.description: Agent actually assigned the property set using the BIM tool's property management interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The property set 'Pset_ZoneCommon' is visible in the standard BlenderBIM property panels, not just typed into a text note or unrelated field.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file through the BlenderBIM export/save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender file menu or export dialog is used to save the IFC file, rather than a terminal 'cp' or 'touch' command creating a dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spec document is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/thermal_zoning_brief.txt'. The required zones to be created are 'Living Zone', 'Sleeping Zone', and 'Unconditioned Zone'. The required property set to attach is 'Pset_ZoneCommon'. The final output file must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_thermal_zones.ifc'. Note: The model only contains 7 IfcSpace entities, which contradicts the brief's claim of 14 rooms. The agent should not be penalized for being unable to assign the exact number of spaces requested in the brief, as long as it assigns the available spaces to the zones.
